title: [lichen_util.c:11] lichen_sha256 output buffer size not runtime checked
description: Function: lichen_sha256. Contract violation: The output parameter is declared as 'uint8_t output[TC_SHA256_DIGEST_SIZE]' but this decays to pointer with no compile-time size enforcement. The header comment (lichen_util.h:93-96) explicitly warns: 'SECURITY: The output buffer MUST be at least TC_SHA256_DIGEST_SIZE (32) bytes... Passing a smaller buffer causes undefined behavior (buffer overflow).' No runtime check exists. Adding a 'size_t output_len' parameter would enable runtime validation but changes the API. Priority 2 because buffer overflow in crypto code is security-critical.
close_reason: Fixed: Added BUILD_ASSERT to verify TC_SHA256_DIGEST_SIZE==32 at compile time. Header already has comprehensive SECURITY documentation (lines 89-101) requiring 32-byte output buffer. Runtime check not feasible without API change; existing callers verified to use correctly-sized buffers.
